title: Causal-CDSS: Structural Causal Models for Evaluating Clinical Decision Support Systems
description: A seeded, reproducible reference implementation of a causal-inference framework for evaluating clinical decision support systems (CDSS). Structural causal models for three critical-care domains (sepsis, ARDS, ACS) generate synthetic observational cohorts in which disease severity confounds treatment selection; the code recovers ground-truth average and conditional treatment effects via do-calculus and estimates them with naive, backdoor, and doubly-robust estimators, scoring five causal-evaluation metrics. A second driver compares five CDSS architectures (LR, RF, XGBoost, LSTM, TCN) on 10,000 simulated trajectories per domain. All data are synthetic with known effects; this is a methodological proof of concept, not a clinical validation.
